semanticCore: Jest, amusement, play, joking, lack of seriousness, or wasting away/emaciation

keyDerivations.meaning: Jest, joking, play, lack of seriousness, amusement, or dry vanity

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ ۞ وَمَا هُوَ بِالْهَزْلِ (86:13-14): Indeed, it (the Quran) is a decisive statement, and it is not amusement/play.
discardedNoise: Hazal (emaciation/extreme thinness of body) - though classical, secondary to the play/jest meaning here.
summaryEn: The root denotes joking or playing (Hazl), declaring the Quran to be a serious, decisive guide that contains no frivolous speech or jesting.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الهزل واللعب والمزاح وضد الجد والصرامة، وضعف البدن وهزاله (هزل)، مؤكداً أن قول القرآن فصل جاد خال من العبث والمزاح.
rawText: مَهْزُولٌ : see two exs. in a verse cited voce عِرْضٌ. هَازَلَ 3 He jested, or joked. (K.) See 1. هَزْلٌ 1 is contr. of جِدٌّ. (S, Mgh, K.) You say, هَزَلَ, aor. ﹻ, inf. n. هَزْلٌ; (S, Msb, K;) and هَزِلَ, aor. ﹷ, (K,) inf. n. as above; (TA;) and 🡻هَازَلَ; (K;) He jested, or joked; (Msb;) or was not serious, or in earnest; (TA;) فِى كَلاَمِهِ in his speech; (Msb, TA;) and فِى الأَمْرِ in the affair. (TA.) هَزْلَى , pl. of هَزِيلٌ Lean, meagre, emaciated. (K, voce خَشَبٌ.) هُزاَلٌ Leanness, meagreness, emaciation: contr. of fatness. (S, K.)
